question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user flood the publish() endpoint with concurrent requests during a network partition so that all threads block on properties.getTimeout(), exhausting the reactive scheduler and causing a full denial-of-service against all other publish operations?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition by saturating the upstream gRPC connection so that the .timeout(properties.getTimeout()) operator fires on every in-flight Mono, causing a cascade of PublishException wrapping that permanently marks all scenarios as errored without recovery?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of retry logic in publish() so that a transient network partition causes every single PublishRequest to fail permanently, with no back-pressure or circuit-breaker preventing unbounded error accumulation in scenario.onError()?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes clients.elementAt(clientIndex) block indefinitely because the cached Flux<Client> was initialized with unreachable nodes, and the cache() operator prevents re-initialization, leading to permanent monitor blindness?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user induce a network partition at the exact moment getClients() is first called so that nodeSupplier.refresh() returns an empty or partial node list, and the resulting Flux<Client> is permanently cached via cache(), making all subsequent publish() calls use stale/unreachable clients forever?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Flux.defer(this::getClients).cache() pattern so that a network partition during initial client construction causes a cached error state, and because cache() replays the terminal signal, all future subscribers immediately receive the error without any re-attempt?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that prevents NodeAddressBook resolution, so that toClient() builds a Client with an empty or single-node network map, and all transactions are routed to a single unreachable node, amplifying the partition impact?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Collectors.toMap() merge function (a, b) -> a in toClient() during a network partition where duplicate endpoints are returned, causing certain nodes to be silently dropped from the network map and concentrating all traffic on fewer nodes, worsening partition effects?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes client.setNetworkFromAddressBook() to receive a stale NodeAddressBook, and since client.setNetwork(network) is called immediately after and overrides it, the address book TLS certificate data is discarded, allowing a man-in-the-middle during reconnection?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause nodeSupplier.refresh() to return nodes with attacker-controlled endpoints (via a poisoned address book API), which are then embedded into the cached Client objects via toClient(), redirecting all future transactions to attacker infrastructure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition so that Mono.fromFuture(executable.executeAsync(client)) never completes, and since the future is not bounded by any internal SDK timeout before the outer .timeout(properties.getTimeout()) fires, the thread holding the future reference leaks until GC, causing memory pressure?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: execute(Client, Transaction)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause Mono.fromCallable(() -> executable.execute(client)) to block the calling thread indefinitely when publishProperties.isAsync() is false, exhausting the bounded thread pool and preventing any new publish operations from being scheduled?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: execute(Client, Query)] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition after the initial transaction succeeds but before processTransactionResponse() calls execute() for the receipt query, so that the receipt Mono hangs until timeout, causing the transaction to be submitted but never confirmed, corrupting scenario success/failure metrics?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that occurs between getTransactionResponse() and processTransactionResponse() so that the TransactionRecordQuery with a fixed 1 HBAR payment is submitted to an unreachable node, causing the HBAR to be deducted without a record being returned, draining the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: processTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger repeated network partitions timed to interrupt processTransactionResponse() after transaction submission, causing the monitor to repeatedly submit transactions (each consuming fees) while never receiving receipts, leading to operator account fund exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes nodeSupplier.get() throw IllegalArgumentException (no valid nodes), and since this exception is thrown inside getTransactionResponse() before onErrorMap() wraps it, the raw exception propagates to scenario.onError() with a different type than PublishException, breaking error classification?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getTransactionResponse()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause nodeSupplier.get() to return a stale node, and since transaction.setNodeAccountIds(node.getAccountIds()) mutates the shared Transaction object, concurrent publish() calls racing on the same transaction instance during partition recovery could corrupt the node routing for multiple in-flight requests?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that secureRandom.nextInt(publishProperties.getClients()) selects from a fixed-size cached client pool, so that during a network partition all clients in the pool become unreachable simultaneously, and there is no mechanism to evict or replace them, causing 100% publish failure rate?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: close()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a network partition that causes close() to be invoked (e.g., via application shutdown triggered by health check failure), and since clients.subscribe(client -> client.close()) is fire-and-forget with exceptions silently swallowed, SDK-level connections are never properly terminated, leaving dangling gRPC channels that consume file descriptors?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: close()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition to cause close() to block indefinitely because client.close() internally waits for in-flight gRPC calls to drain, and since those calls are stuck due to the partition, the JVM shutdown hook hangs, preventing clean process termination and causing the monitor to be forcibly killed with data loss?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the .onErrorMap(t -> !(t instanceof PublishException), t -> new PublishException(request, t)) operator so that during a network partition, TimeoutException is wrapped in PublishException and the original timeout cause is obscured, preventing operators from distinguishing partition-induced timeouts from application-level errors in monitoring dashboards?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network partition that makes the reactive pipeline in publish() emit errors faster than scenario.onError() can process them, causing unbounded growth of the errors collection in PublishScenario and eventually triggering an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the monitor process?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: getClients()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition during getClients() initialization so that Flux.range(0, publishProperties.getClients()).flatMap(i -> Mono.defer(() -> Mono.just(toClient(nodes)))) creates clients with an empty nodes list, and toClient() with empty nodes produces a Client with no network endpoints, causing all subsequent execute() calls to fail with a non-descriptive SDK exception?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: toClient()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a network partition that causes nodeSupplier.refresh() to return nodes with mismatched accountIds and endpoints, so that the Collectors.toMap() in toClient() silently deduplicates them via (a, b) -> a, resulting in a network map where some AccountIds are mapped to wrong endpoints, causing transactions to be routed incorrectly after partition recovery?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/publish/TransactionPublisher.java -> Scope: Critical: Network partition caused outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a sustained network partition that keeps properties.getTimeout() firing repeatedly, and since each timeout generates a new PublishException wrapping a TimeoutException, the scenario error map grows unboundedly with duplicate timeout entries, consuming heap memory proportional to the publish rate times the partition duration?
